title: Alfaro celebrates that Sheinbaum opens the possibility of reviewing the Fiscal Pact
text:
The Governor of‚Äč the State, Enrique Alfaro Ramirez celebrated that the President of‚ÄĆ Mexico, Claudia Sheinbaum Pardo would open the possibility of reviewing the fiscal pact ‚ÄĆand open a channel where the important points of‚Äć the same can be ‚ĀĘdiscussed, as well as the opening of a discussion table.
The president said that Jalisco‚ĀĘ does ‚Äčnot want to‚Ā£ get out of the fiscal pact, but ‚Ā§he does want‚Ā£ to review a document that has become obsolete ‚ĀĘand does not‚Äč reflect fair remuneration to ‚ĀĘone of the states that contributes the most to the national ‚Ā§economy.

‚ÄúI think it‚Äč is very important that the President ‚Ā§has put on the table the need and the possibility of reviewing ‚ÄĆthe Fiscal Pact because perhaps ‚Ā§she may not share the ‚Ā§idea that Jalisco puts the exit route‚Ā§ on the‚Ā£ table, ‚Äčbut‚ÄĆ I would like to take the opportunity to insist and tell the President that what we ‚Äćwant is to build a new fiscal agreement; that Jalisco does not want to leave the Fiscal Pact, but what has happened is that, given the lack of will‚Ā£ of the Ministry of Finance to establish a table for the reconstruction of the Fiscal Pact, they have‚ÄĆ left us no ‚Ā£other‚Äč option ‚Ā§than to follow that path.‚ÄĚ
Alfaro Ram√≠rez said that he hopes that the President can open a discussion table, which ‚Äćwould be taking a historic step.
‚ÄúIf the President proposes a respectful‚ĀĘ and institutional‚Äč dialog table, I ‚ÄĆthink she would be taking a historic step. I am confident that this can happen, and I am going to look for the opportunity to personally discuss the issue ‚ĀĘwith her, because it is very important that ‚Ā£the President knows that what we are putting‚Ā§ on the table is not with a disruptive spirit, but ‚ĀĘas ‚Äčan action in the face ‚Ā£of ‚ÄĆthe Treasury‚Äôs refusal to generate a dialog that ‚ÄĆwould allow the Fiscal Pact‚Äć to be improved.‚ÄĆ ‚ÄúI believe‚Ā§ that what the President said today is something that encourages me, as I‚Ā£ understand ‚Äćthat there is a need to review an agreement that was‚Äć made many‚Ā§ years ago and that I believe no longer meets‚ÄĆ the needs of this‚Äč country,‚ÄĚ he stated. the Governor.

Time.News Editor‚ÄĆ (TNE): Good afternoon, and welcome ‚Ā£to our exclusive interview. Today,‚Äč we‚Äôre delving ‚Ā§into a‚ÄĆ significant ‚ÄĆdevelopment in ‚ÄĆMexican fiscal ‚Äćpolicy. With us is Dr. ‚ÄĆLaura Mart√≠nez, an expert ‚Äćin public finance and state economics. Dr. Mart√≠nez, thank you for joining us.
Dr. Laura‚Äć Mart√≠nez (DLM): Thank ‚ÄĆyou‚ĀĘ for having me. It‚Äôs a‚ĀĘ pleasure to be here.
TNE: ‚Ā§To start off, let‚Äôs discuss‚Ā§ the recent statements by Jalisco‚Äôs Governor, Enrique Alfaro Ramirez, regarding the fiscal pact. He expressed ‚Äćsatisfaction about President Claudia Sheinbaum ‚Ā§Pardo‚Äôs openness to reviewing the‚Äč pact.‚Äč Why is this review ‚Ā§important, especially ‚Äćfor‚Ā£ Jalisco?
DLM: This review‚ÄĆ is critical for Jalisco because it highlights the state‚Äôs significant contributions to the national economy. As one of ‚ÄĆthe leading states ‚ÄĆin terms of economic output, many local leaders believe that the current fiscal pact does not adequately ‚Ā§reflect Jalisco‚Äôs contributions. This puts Jalisco at‚Äć a disadvantage in terms of funding for essential services and development.
TNE: Interesting. Governor‚Äč Alfaro mentioned‚Ā§ that‚Äč Jalisco does not want to exit the fiscal pact but wishes‚Äč to review it ‚Äčdue ‚ĀĘto‚ÄĆ its obsolescence. What factors contribute to a fiscal‚Ā£ pact becoming outdated?
DLM: ‚ÄĆ Several factors ‚Äčcan lead to a fiscal pact becoming outdated. Economic landscapes change‚ÄĒnew industries emerge,‚ÄĆ population shifts occur, and the cost of living fluctuates. Additionally, if a pact‚Äč fails‚Äč to‚Ā£ account ‚Ā§for these changes, it can‚Äč create imbalances where certain states do not receive adequate funding relative to their economic contributions. This is the situation Jalisco finds itself in.
TNE: In opening a dialog, what are some key points ‚ĀĘthat ‚ÄĆthe ‚Ā£Governor would likely‚Äć want to address during these discussions?
DLM: He will likely want to address equitable distribution of resources, considering factors like population ‚Ā£growth, economic performance, and infrastructure needs. ‚ÄĆThere ‚ĀĘmay also be a push for more flexible funding mechanisms‚Äć that allow states to respond dynamically to changing circumstances, such as natural disasters or economic downturns.
TNE: President Sheinbaum‚Äôs ‚Ā£willingness to open a dialog is encouraging, but ‚ĀĘwhat challenges ‚ÄĆmight arise in ‚Ā£these discussions?
DLM: One challenge ‚ÄĆcould be the varying interests of different states. Each state has its unique needs and economic contexts, and‚ĀĘ consensus can be difficult to achieve. Furthermore, political dynamics could play a‚Äć significant role‚ÄĒbalancing‚Ā§ the interests of the federal government with local‚Äč state‚Ā§ aspirations is often delicate.
TNE: ‚ÄćAnd how could these discussions impact ‚Ā§citizens,‚ÄĆ particularly in Jalisco?
DLM: If successful, a revised ‚ĀĘfiscal‚Äć pact could lead to increased investments in public services, infrastructure, and social programs in Jalisco. This would directly benefit‚Ā£ citizens, enhancing their quality of life ‚Ā§and potentially stimulating further economic growth.‚Ā§ Conversely, if discussions‚ĀĘ don‚Äôt lead‚Äć to tangible improvements,‚Äć it ‚ĀĘcould ‚Äćexacerbate‚Äč existing inequalities.
TNE: Dr. Mart√≠nez, what‚Ā£ do you foresee ‚Äćas the potential long-term implications of these discussions for Mexico‚Äôs overall fiscal‚ĀĘ policy?
DLM: If this process leads to a‚Äć successful revision, it could set a precedent for more dynamic, responsive fiscal policies across Mexico. This could result in a re-evaluation ‚Äčof how resources are allocated nationwide, ensuring‚Ā£ that‚Ā§ contributions are fairly recognized and compensated. However, it‚Äôs crucial for these discussions ‚ĀĘto be conducted ‚Ā§transparently and inclusively to promote trust among different states.
TNE: Thank you so much for your insights today, Dr. Mart√≠nez. ‚ĀĘIt will be fascinating to see how these‚ÄĆ discussions unfold and what they mean for Jalisco ‚ĀĘand the rest of Mexico.
